description_long: This house features a low-pitched, dark gray hip roof with multiple intersecting sections, giving it a modern yet understated appearance. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored horizontal siding, providing a clean and neutral look. Windows are rectangular and symmetrically arranged on most sides, with dark trim that contrasts against the light facade. The main entryway is marked by a single door with a subtle design, set beneath a small overhang that offers minimal shelter. There are no visible porches, columns, or decorative elements, emphasizing simplicity and function. The overall structure is compact and single-storied, suitable for suburban or urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a dark hip roof, light horizontal siding, and symmetrically arranged rectangular windows. The design is simple and modern, with a minimal entryway and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: This view shows the main entry door beneath a small overhang, with two windows on adjacent walls and the intersecting hip roof sections clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side displays three evenly spaced windows and the continuous expanse of the hip roof, with no visible doors or entry features.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the rear of the house, featuring two windows on the main wall and one on the side extension, with the roof's intersecting lines prominent.
description_view_3: This view presents the secondary entry door centered between two windows, with the hip roof sloping down symmetrically on both sides.
attribute_entryway_design: Single door with minimal overhang, no porch or decorative elements
